stem: A clinical study study measures the diastolic blood pressure (mmHg) for a sample of 20 patients. The sample mean is 213.1 mmHg with a sample standard deviation of 38.1 mmHg. However, 2 of the 20 observations appear to be outliers (values more than 2.5 standard deviations from the mean). If these outliers are removed, the remaining 18 observations have a mean of 206.5 mmHg and standard deviation of 23.5 mmHg. Estimate the true population mean and provide a 95% confidence interval that accounts for the uncertainty about whether outliers should be included.

ground_truth.answer: With all data: mean = 213.1, 95% CI = [195.3, 230.9]. Without outliers: mean = 206.5, 95% CI = [195.4, 217.6]. Combined interval accounting for analytic uncertainty: [195.3, 230.9]. Point estimate: 209.8 mmHg.

solution_steps: The outlier treatment choice creates structural uncertainty. Combined interval: [195.3, 230.9].
common_failure_modes: Using only the full sample without considering outlier impact
Removing outliers without reporting both analyses
Not recognizing that the choice of analysis method is itself a source of uncertainty
